name: Alcalá de la Selva - Peña Ojerada Loop
name_original: Alcalá de la Selva - Peña Ojerada
description: Hike the moderate 4.8-mile (7.7 km) Alcala de la Selva - Peña Ojerada trail in Sierra de Gúdar, featuring panoramic views and a unique rock
region_intro: Hiking around Alcalá De La Selva offers diverse terrain within Spain's Sierra de Gúdar mountains, situated at over 1400 meters altitude. The region is characterized by rugged landscapes, pine forests, and deep ravines carved by rivers like the Alcalá. Hikers can explore a variety of environments, from riverside paths to high mountain areas with peaks like Portillo Ramiro. Best hiking trails around Alcalá De La Selva The most popular hiking route is Alcalá de la Selva - Peña Ojerada Loop, a 4.8 miles (7.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 23 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the surrounding mountainous landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Alcalá de la Selva Castle – Peñarroya Summit Viewpoint loop from Alcalá de la Selva, a difficult 10.3 miles (16.7 km) path. This challenging route features significant elevation gain, leading to panoramic viewpoints. Local hikers also love the Alcalá de la Selva Castle – Alcalá de la Selva loop from Alcalá de la Selva, a 3.3 miles (5.3 km) trail leading through the local area, often completed in about 1 hour 25 minutes. Hiking around Alcalá De La Selva is defined by its Sierra de Gúdar mountain environment, extensive pine forests, and picturesque river ravines. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to more demanding ascents. The routes in Alcalá De La Selva are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Alcalá De La Selva's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Alcalá de la Selva?
A: There are over 15 hiking trails around Alcalá de la Selva, offering a diverse range of experiences within the Sierra de Gúdar mountains. These routes cater to various fitness levels, from easy strolls to more demanding ascents.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Alcalá de la Selva?
A: The Sierra de Gúdar region offers unique hiking experiences throughout the year. Spring is ideal for wildflowers in high mountain meadows, autumn for mushroom picking and vibrant foliage, and winter transforms the landscape into a snowy wonderland suitable for snowshoeing. Summer provides pleasant temperatures at higher altitudes.

Q: What are the different difficulty levels of hiking trails in Alcalá de la Selva?
A: The trails around Alcalá de la Selva offer a good mix of difficulties. You'll find 2 easy routes perfect for a relaxed walk, 13 moderate trails that provide a good challenge, and 3 difficult routes for experienced hikers seeking significant elevation gain and longer distances.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls or river scenery?
A: Yes, the region is rich in water features. You can explore the majestic natural scenery of the Barranco del Nacimiento and Cañón Río Alcalá. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981392610/alcala-de-la-selva-pena-ojerada-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de la Selva - Peña Ojerada Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, offers views of the surrounding mountainous landscape, often near water sources. The Cascada de la Hiedra (Ivy Waterfall) is also a beautiful natural formation popular with nature lovers.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while hiking in the Sierra de Gúdar?
A: Hikers in the Sierra de Gúdar will encounter diverse landscapes including extensive pine forests (Scots pine, black pine), deep ravines carved by rivers like the Alcalá, and high mountain environments with meadows and pastures. Peaks such as Portillo Ramiro (1,754m) and Monte Negro (1,715m) offer more challenging routes and stunning vistas.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to explore along the trails?
A: Absolutely. Many trails pass by significant historical sites. The medieval &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5359402" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de la Selva Castle&lt;/a&gt;, perched on a rocky hill, is a prominent landmark. You can also find the Sanctuary of the Virgen de la Vega and the Ermita de Loreto, offering glimpses into local history and culture amidst natural settings. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14709625" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de la Selva Castle – Peñarroya Summit Viewpoint loop&lt;/a&gt; for a route that includes historical points and panoramic views.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints on the trails?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain provides numerous opportunities for breathtaking views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5805644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya Summit and Observation Tower&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic vistas. Additionally, the Mirador del Castillejo is a recommended stop for its tranquility and astonishing views of the landscape, perfect for relaxing after an ascent.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Alcalá de la Selva?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14709816" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de la Selva Castle – Alcalá de la Selva loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.3-mile trail that takes you through the local area. The Cañón del Río Alcalá also offers an 8-kilometer circular route between Cabra de Mora and Alcalá de la Selva.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Alcalá de la Selva?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning natural landscapes, the tranquility of the Sierra de Gúdar, and the diverse terrain that offers options for all abilities, from riverside paths to high mountain ascents.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Alcalá de la Selva?
A: Yes, the region offers trails suitable for families. The Senda Fluvial del Río Alcalá provides serene riverside walks, and the Fuente del Nacimiento is a charming natural refuge with trails suitable for all levels of hikers, including those with children. There are 2 easy routes available, perfect for a family outing.

Q: Is it possible to hike to a mountain refuge in the area?
A: Yes, you can hike to mountain refuges. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5285224" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Chaparrilla Refuge&lt;/a&gt; is a notable point of interest in the area. A challenging route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14709645" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Chaparrilla Refuge – Peñarroya Summit Viewpoint loop&lt;/a&gt; starts from Urbanización Sol y Nieve and leads you through the high mountain environment.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Alcalá de la Selva?
A: While specific regulations for every trail can vary, generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in natural areas like the Sierra de Gúdar, especially if kept on a leash. It's always recommended to check local signage or specific trail guidelines for any restrictions, particularly in protected areas or during certain seasons.
tips: Alcalá de la Selva is a charming municipality in the province of Teruel, Aragon. Of Arab origin, it offers a stunning view of the town.

The Peña Ojerada in Alcalá de la Selva is a popular destination for hiking enthusiasts. One of the most recommended routes is the Circular Route of the Viewpoints, which includes the Peña Ojerada and Monte Pinar.

faqs.question: Is the Alcala de la Selva - Peña Ojerada trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is classified as moderate and is generally suitable for all skill levels, though a good physical condition is recommended. It covers a distance of 7.66 kilometers with an elevation gain of 270 meters, typically taking around 2 hours and 23 minutes to complete.

faqs.question: Is the trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The Alcala de la Selva - Peña Ojerada trail is a circular route, offering varied perspectives throughout your journey and bringing you back to your starting point.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail starts in Alcalá de la Selva. While specific parking details aren't provided, as a popular starting point for hikes, you can generally find parking within or near the village.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail winds through the scenic Sierra de Gúdar mountains, featuring generally easily-accessible paths. You'll experience varied landscapes, from pine forests to potentially cultivated fields, with views of limestone cliffs and dramatic canyons in the broader area.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route, especially at Peña Ojerada?
faqs.answer: The main highlight is the distinctive Peña Ojerada Rock Formation, which translates to 'perforated rock' or 'rock with holes'. This natural monument offers panoramic views. You'll also pass through the charming village of Alcalá de la Selva and enjoy the diverse mountain scenery of the Sierra de Gúdar.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Gúdar region is vibrant throughout the year. Spring is excellent for wild lilies, autumn for mushrooms, and winter can offer beautiful snowy landscapes. The trail is generally accessible, but check local weather conditions, especially for winter hikes.

faqs.question: Is the Alcala de la Selva - Peña Ojerada trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this trail aren't detailed, trails in the Sierra de Gúdar are generally dog-friendly. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other hikers, and to carry waste bags.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail or the Sierra de Gúdar area?
faqs.answer: There is no mention of specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking the Alcala de la Selva - Peña Ojerada trail or for accessing the general Sierra de Gúdar area. You can typically enjoy the natural beauty freely.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths in the region?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other established trails. You'll find it passes through parts of the Sierra de Gúdar, MontañasVacías, Senda del Maestrazgo, and Senda Fluvial trails.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, restaurants, or accommodation options nearby?
faqs.answer: The trail starts in Alcalá de la Selva, a village that is likely to offer local amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options for hikers and visitors.

faqs.question: What should I bring or wear for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and varied terrain, comfortable hiking shoes are essential. Dress in layers suitable for mountain weather, and bring water, snacks, sun protection, and a map or GPS device. If hiking in winter, be prepared for colder temperatures and potential snow.
